beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee and breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Jack starts the day with full energy on a sunny Monday; he's a morning person and will use the good weather to his advantage later.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland High School for work day.
beats.reasoning: Jack commutes from North Seattle to Kirkland; 40 min drive is standard for this route during light Monday morning traffic.

beats.activity: Teach science classes: first two periods of the day.
beats.reasoning: Jack's teaching schedule runs through mid-morning; he's passionate about his classes and won't skip them for the FIFA match at 11am.

beats.activity: Lunch break at school; grabs a quick bite rather than watching the USA vs Wales match.
beats.reasoning: Jack is a focused teacher and won't duck out to a bar for the 11am FIFA match; he prioritizes his work responsibilities over the event and eats at school.
Jack continues his teaching day through the afternoon; he's engaged with his students and finishes out the school day.
Jack heads home after school; reverse commute is slightly faster, ~45 min to get back to North Seattle.

beats.activity: Stop at local grocery for foraging gear and snacks.
beats.reasoning: Jack's curious about mushrooms and wants to pick up supplies; the sunny weather makes today perfect for a foraging walk, and Wallingford is his neighborhood.

beats.activity: Go foraging for mushrooms at Gas Works Park, enjoy the sunny weather.
beats.reasoning: Perfect conditions for Jack's foraging hobby: sunny, warm (74F), and a Monday evening means fewer crowds; he values this kind of solo exploration time.

beats.activity: Head to a favorite bagel spot for dinner with a close friend.
beats.reasoning: Jack has strong opinions about bagels and prefers time with close friends over crowds; meeting one friend for a casual dinner is in character.

beats.activity: Walk around University District with friend, then head home.
beats.reasoning: Jack enjoys the warm evening and spends time exploring the neighborhood with his friend before wrapping up the day.

beats.activity: Drive home, relax at home, wind down with a book or podcast.
beats.reasoning: Jack returns home and decompresses after a full day of teaching, foraging, and social time; he's ready to settle in for the night.
Jack gets a solid 8 hours of sleep after a balanced, active day; the sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary:
Started the day in a groove—coffee hit different this morning. Taught two solid periods of chemistry, and honestly, the kids were engaged today. Skipped the World Cup buzz at lunch; felt right to stay focused on grading papers instead of the bar scene downtown.

After school, I bolted home and grabbed some gear at Whole Foods, then headed straight to Gas Works Park. The weather was pristine—74 degrees, not a cloud. Found some decent oyster mushrooms near the old industrial structures; there's something about foraging that just quiets my mind. Met up with Sarah at Blazer Bob's afterward for bagels and caught up on everything. We wandered through the U-District for a bit, grabbed coffee, watched the sunset start creeping in. Felt genuinely present, you know? Got home, cracked open a book, and now I'm ready to sleep.
thought_process: I made a conscious choice to skip the World Cup watch—part of me wanted the social energy, but I knew I'd be more centered staying at school. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I drove, which was lucky. The sunny weather was calling me toward something restorative, so foraging felt like the right move instead of hitting downtown bars. By evening, I needed human connection again, so meeting Sarah balanced everything out. Today felt like I was listening to what I actually needed rather than what I thought I should do.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
